seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between No 5 Branch and No 6 Branch. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: No 5 Branch vs No 6 Branch: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in No 5 Branch and No 6 Branch.
heading: No 5 Branch vs No 6 Branch — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between No 5 Branch, Queensland and No 6 Branch, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: No 5 Branch has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to No 6 Branch (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is No 5 Branch or No 6 Branch safer from flooding?
faq.answer: No 5 Branch has a flood risk score of 37/100, while No 6 Branch scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: No 5 Branch scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. No 6 Branch scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in No 5 Branch or No 6 Branch?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. No 5 Branch has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
